creator: gjb@FreeBSD.org
text:
On Mon, Jul 23, 2012 at 10:27:54PM -0400, Randy Pratt wrote:
> On Sun, 22 Jul 2012 15:27:09 GMT
> gjb@FreeBSD.org wrote:
> 
> > Synopsis: Mailing list archives missing for some svn groups
> > 
> > Responsible-Changed-From-To: freebsd-www->gjb
> > Responsible-Changed-By: gjb
> > Responsible-Changed-When: Sun Jul 22 15:25:54 UTC 2012
> > Responsible-Changed-Why: 
> > The archives are updated every week, so I am not convinced there is a
> > problem with the svn-port-* lists, since it is exactly a week since the
> > conversion from CVS.
> > 
> > I do not know about svn-soc-all@ yet.
> > 
> > 
> > http://www.freebsd.org/cgi/query-pr.cgi?pr=170065
> 
> Yes, you are correct.  The mailing list archives are created every
> Sunday at 00:00 UTC and on December 31.  It usually takes 4 to 5 hours
> for the completed archives to show up.  This can be ascertained by the
> date stamps of last week's archives:
> 
>   http://docs.freebsd.org/mail/archive/2012/
> 
> Archiving is date-based and not dependent on the age of the list.
> 
> The absence of the 3 svn groups is a pretty good indication that their
> archives for the week ending 7/22 are not present.  I have no way of
> telling if it is missing links on the web page or if the archives have
> not been created.
> 
> There were 443 messages posted to svn-ports-all as of 7/22 at 23:55 UTC
> and they disappeared at 00:05 UTC.  This is normal for the archiving
> period.  Where did these messages go?
> 

To be honest, I'm not sure.

It seems to me we (-www@) go through this similar problem every time a
new list is added, so I will prod the appropriate entities.

Thanks for the report.

Glen
